message:
fix(core,journal): zero partial-block tail on truncate to prevent data leakage; reject duplicate/post-commit COW records

- Add partial-block tail zeroing in setattr truncate path: when the new
  file size does not align to block_size, read the partial trailing block,
  fill bytes from new_size%block_size to end with zeros, and write it
  back — prevents stale data beyond the logical EOF from being exposed
  on subsequent reads or extent reuse
- Add committed sequence tracking to recover_native_cow: maintain a
  BTreeSet of committed sequences, rejecting Write records that reference
  an already-committed sequence (stops replay from appending late writes
  to a closed transaction) and deduplicating Commit records
- Add native_cow_duplicate_commit_same_sequence_recovered_once test
- Add native_cow_write_after_commit_same_sequence_is_discarded test
- Replace closure with PoisonError::into_inner in test lock helper
- Replace set_default with with_default in fallocate log-contract test
  for cleaner subscriber scoping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
